ddls:
CREATE TABLE "schools" (
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each school */,
  "school_name" TEXT /* Name of the school */,
  "local_authority_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the local authority the school belongs to */,
  "school_type_id" INTEGER /* Type of school (e.g., Academy converter, Academy sponsor led) */,
  "number_of_pupils" REAL /* Number of pupils in the school */,
  "allocated_amount" REAL /* Allocated amount for the school */,
  "school_address" TEXT /* Address of the school */,
  "school_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the school */,
  "school_email" TEXT /* Email address of the school */,
  "headteacher_name" TEXT /* Name of the headteacher */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("school_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_schools_local_authority_id FOREIGN KEY ("local_authority_id") REFERENCES local_authorities ("local_authority_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_schools_school_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_type_id") REFERENCES school_types ("school_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "local_authorities" (
  "local_authority_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each local authority */,
  "local_authority_name" TEXT /* Name of the local authority */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the local authority */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number for inquiries about the local authority */,
  "local_authority_address" TEXT /* Address of the local authority */,
  "local_authority_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the local authority */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("local_authority_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "school_types" (
  "school_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each school type */,
  "school_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the school type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the school type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("school_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "pupils" (
  "pupil_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each pupil */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* ID of the school the pupil belongs to */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the pupil */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the pupil */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the pupil */,
  "special_needs" INTEGER /* Whether the pupil has special needs */,
  "special_needs_details" TEXT /* Additional information about the pupil's special needs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("pupil_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_pupils_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "funding_allocations" (
  "funding_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each funding allocation */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* ID of the school the funding is allocated to */,
  "allocated_amount" REAL /* Allocated amount for the school */,
  "additional_funding" REAL /* Additional funding provided to the school */,
  "allocation_date" TEXT /* Date the funding was allocated */,
  "funding_source" TEXT /* Source of the funding */,
  "funding_purpose" TEXT /* Purpose of the funding */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("funding_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_funding_allocations_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "funding_history" (
  "funding_history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each funding history entry */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* ID of the school the funding history belongs to */,
  "allocated_amount" REAL /* Allocated amount for the school at a specific point in time */,
  "allocation_date" TEXT /* Date the funding was allocated */,
  "funding_status" TEXT /* Status of the funding */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("funding_history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_funding_history_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., Local Authority Admin, School Admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_schools" (
  "user_school_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-school relationship */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* ID of the school the user has access to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user for the school (e.g., read-only, edit, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_school_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_schools_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_schools_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)
